Notice Of Motion And Motion To Deem Requests For Admission Admitted
Off Calendar Jan-30-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF WESTFIELD 816-818 MISSION STREET LLC's, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY, Motion To Deem Requests For Admission Admitted.

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel, or Interested Person. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Plaintiff's Motion to Deem Requests for Admission Admitted is granted. As of date, Defendants have failed to respond to Plaintiff's Request for Admissions. The failure to timely respond results in waiver of all objections to the requests, including claims of privilege or work product protection. (Code of Civil Proc. Sec. 2033.280, subdivision (a).) "[W]oe betide the party who fails to serve responses before the hearing. In that instance the court has no discretion but to grant the admission motion, usually with fatal consequences for the defaulting party. One might call it "two strikes and you're out" as applied to civil procedure. (Demyer v. Costa Mesa Mobile Home Estates (1995) 36 Cal.App.4th 393, 395.) As such, the admissions are deemed admitted. (Code of Civil Proc. Sec. 2033.280, subdivision (c).) Plaintiff is awarded attorney's fees in the amount of $1,412.50 ($565 x 2.5 hours). (Ibid., Code of Civil Proc. Sec. 2023.010(d) and (i), 2023.020, 2023.030(a).)) Monetary sanctions in this context are mandatory. (Code of Civil Proc. Sec. 2033.280(c).)

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must deliver it to Dept. 301.

Judge: Christopher C. Hite. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported. (301/CCH)
